text.title: Communications Management Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding Communications Management. Each pairs a binary check and a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each stakeholder group MUST have its own row in the communications matrix specifying artefact, channel, owner, cadence, and format.
rule.rationale: Stakeholder-agnostic comms produce a blast to all; the wrong people get the wrong artefacts at the wrong cadence.

rule.statement: Decisions MUST be appended to an immutable decision log with id, date, decision, options-considered, rationale, and decider.
rule.rationale: Without an immutable log, decisions re-litigate forever; new joiners can't reconstruct project history.

rule.statement: Status reports MUST be issued on a fixed published cadence (weekly / biweekly / monthly). Ad-hoc reporting is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Variable cadence trains stakeholders to ignore reports; fixed cadence makes silence informative.

rule.statement: Every meeting MUST have at least one decision or a 'no decision needed' note in the minutes. Meetings without decisions trigger a cadence review.
rule.rationale: Decision-free meetings burn calendar without output; the rule forces either decisions or cadence reduction.

rule.statement: Each communication channel MUST have a named escalation path: who is contacted when the primary owner doesn't respond within the SLA window.
rule.rationale: Unnamed escalation means contributors choose ad-hoc paths; quiet failures compound.

rule.statement: If preconditions for Communications Management fail (see Skip-If list in AGENTS.md), the operator MUST skip this methodology and document the fallback reason instead of fabricating an output.
rule.rationale: Applying Communications Management outside its envelope produces false-precision artefacts. Explicit skip preserves epistemic discipline.
